baseline_candidates.directness: adjacent closest published family, not direct metric-caliber compiler
baseline_candidates.name: AutoLink
baseline_candidates.release_action: report evidence-labeled E3 candidate-linking diagnostics and resource-gated upstream audit

baseline_candidates.directness: adjacent safety system, not denominator/grain witness compiler
baseline_candidates.name: SafeNLIDB
baseline_candidates.release_action: report ShieldSQL guard transfer and resource-gated upstream audit

baseline_candidates.directness: direct for refusal policy, partial for metric-caliber
baseline_candidates.name: TrustSQL-style abstention
baseline_candidates.release_action: map as refusal baseline family; release includes TrustSQL raw official scorer outputs

baseline_candidates.directness: strong SQL baselines, indirect for governed metric contracts
baseline_candidates.name: CHESS / MAC-SQL / DIN-SQL
baseline_candidates.release_action: rank as next SQL-planner controls with oracle-candidate finalizer interface

baseline_candidates.directness: direct mechanism controls under released contracts
baseline_candidates.name: Semantic-layer validator / SQL post-hoc validator
baseline_candidates.release_action: keep as primary reproducible non-witness comparisons

benchmark_candidates.name: BIS
benchmark_candidates.paper_action: discuss as closest external BI benchmark; use release DataBench/Spider2-DBT evidence as executed adjacent anchors
benchmark_candidates.why_not_primary_yet: does not directly release CaliberGraph-style metric-contract witness labels
benchmark_candidates.why_relevant: production BI questions rather than generic academic SQL only

benchmark_candidates.name: BI-Bench
benchmark_candidates.paper_action: use to position the BI system boundary and motivate broader evaluation
benchmark_candidates.why_not_primary_yet: benchmarks BI system insight quality rather than typed metric-caliber witness conformance
benchmark_candidates.why_relevant: descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive BI query coverage

benchmark_candidates.name: TrustSQL
benchmark_candidates.paper_action: add as refusal/reliability comparison axis; release includes raw official scorer outputs
benchmark_candidates.why_not_primary_yet: refusal is one contract facet; it lacks denominator, grain, coverage, and metric-caliber witnesses
benchmark_candidates.why_relevant: scores feasible SQL generation and infeasible-question abstention

benchmark_candidates.name: Spider 2.0 / Spider2-Lite
benchmark_candidates.paper_action: retain as Text-to-SQL diagnostic, not the main metric-caliber benchmark
benchmark_candidates.why_not_primary_yet: schema/workflow success does not define governed metric contracts or disclosure policy
benchmark_candidates.why_relevant: real-world enterprise workflows over large schemas and warehouses

benchmark_candidates.name: BIRD
benchmark_candidates.why_not_primary_yet: does not provide complete governance graph, refusal policy, or coverage contract
benchmark_candidates.why_relevant: public SQL benchmark with aggregate queries and external knowledge

claim_boundary: This historical release audit maps adjacent BI, reliability, and Text-to-SQL benchmarks to the MetricCaliberBench evidence boundary. release supplements it with executed Spider2-DBT, TrustSQL raw, DataBench, and MetricFlow evidence in EXTERNAL_EVIDENCE_SUMMARY.md.
release_decision: Numerical result tables remain limited to released/rebuildable MetricCaliberBench splits and evidence-labeled controls.
External benchmarks are cited and mapped to specific evaluation roles, avoiding false SOTA claims.
release executes adjacent checks for Spider2-DBT, TrustSQL raw, DataBench, and dbt MetricFlow; those checks remain boundary evidence, not full NL2Metric-Caliber witness benchmarks.
